title: neurolepis virgata
intro: Neurolepis virgata, also known as "Quebrada", is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America, specifically in Peru and Bolivia, characterized by its fragrant leaves and stems. Traditionally, Neurolepis virgata has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also known for its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in digestion. Neurolepis virgata can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
